import org.slf4j.Logger;

// JV008 positive: a secret-named value reaching a tier-1 slf4j sink (error/warn)
// as an argument, in every shape the deep scan must reach - a plain name,
// `+` concatenation, a String.format arg, a `this.` field access, and a getter
// call. Not catch-scoped: these are plain methods, so only JV008 fires. The
// receiver name never participates - recognition is by the Logger origin.
class Jv008Slf4jSecret {
    private final Logger log;
    private final Cfg cfg;
    private String apiKey;

    Jv008Slf4jSecret(Logger log) {
        this.log = log;
    }

    void direct(String token) {
        log.error("boom", token); // want "JV008.*token"
    }

    void concat(String authToken) {
        log.error("ctx=" + authToken); // want "JV008.*authToken"
    }

    void formatted(String secretValue) {
        log.error(String.format("k=%s", secretValue)); // want "JV008.*secretValue"
    }

    void fieldAccess() {
        log.warn("read config", this.apiKey); // want "JV008.*apiKey"
    }

    void getterCall() {
        log.error("read config", cfg.getSecret()); // want "JV008.*getSecret"
    }

    interface Cfg {
        String getSecret();
    }
}
